title: Treaty on the Southeast Asia Nuclear Weapon-Free Zone
text:
TREATY ON
' "
THE SOUTHEAST ASIA NUCLEAR WEAPON-FREE 2ONE
The States Parties to this Treaty:
i\
DESIRING to contribute to the realization of the purposes
and principles of the Charter of the United Nations;
DETERMINED to take concrete action which will contribute to
the progress towards general d n a complete disarmament f o
nuclear weapons, e h t o t d n a promotion f o international peace
and security;
REAFFIRMING the desire of the Southeast Asian States to
maintain peace and stability in the region in the spirit of
peaceful coexistence and mutual understanding and
cooperation as enunciated in various communiques,
declarations and other legal instruments;
RECALLING the Declaration on the Zone of Peace, Freedom and
Neutrality (ZOPFAN) signed in Kuala Lumpur on 27 November
1971 and the Programme of Action on ZOPFAN adopted at the
26th ASEAN Ministerial Meeting in Singapore in July 1993;
CONVINCED that the establishment of a Southeast Asia Nuclear
Weapon-Free Zone, n a s a essential component e h t f o ZOPFAN,
will contribute towards strengthening e h t security f o States
within the Zone and towards enhancing international peace
and security as a whole;
REAFFIRMING the importance the of Treaty the on
Non-Proliferation f o Nuclear Weapons (NPT) n i preventing e h t
proliferation of nuclear weapons and in contributing towards
international peace and security;
RECALLING Article VII of the NPT which recognizes the right
of any group of States to conclude regional treaties in
order to assure the total absence of nuclear weapons in
their respective territories;
RECALLING the Final Document of the Tenth Special Session of
e h t United Nations General Assembly which encourages e h t
establishment f o nuclear weapon-free zones;

1 tf RECALLING the. Principles and- Objectives r o f Nuclear
Non-Proliferation d n a Disarmament, adopted t a the. 1995
Review and Extension Conference of the Parties to the NPT,
that the cooperation of all the nuclear-weapon States and
their respect d n a support e h t r o f relevant protocols s i
important for e h t maximum effectiveness f o this nuclear
weapon-free zone treaty .and s t i relevant protocols, ;
DETERMINED to protect the region from environmental
pollution and the hazards posed by radioactive wastes and
other radioactive material;
HAVE AGREED as follows:
Article i
USE OF TERMS
For the purposes of this Treaty and its Protocol:
(a) "Southeast Asia Nuclear Weapon-Free Zone",
hereinafter referred to as the "Zone", means the
area comprising the territories of all states in
Southeast Asia, namely, Brunei Darussalam,
Cambodia, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar,
Philippines, Singapore, Thailand and Vietnam, and
their respective continental shelves and
Exclusive Economic Zones (EEZ);
(b) "territory" means the land territory, internal
waters, territorial sea, archipelagic waters/ the
seabed and the sub-soil thereof and the airspace
above.them;
(c) "nuclear weapon" means any explos ive device
capable of releasing nuclear energy in an
uncontrolled manner but does not include the
means f o transport r o delivery f o such device f i
separable from and not an indivisible part
thereof; -
) d ( "station" means o t deploy, emplace, iinplant,
install, stockpile or store;

3 -
(e) "radioactive material" means material that
contains radionuclides above clearance or
exemption levels recommended by the International
Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA);
(f) "radioactive wastes" means material that contains
or s i contaminated with radionuclides t a
concentrations or activities greater than
clearance levels recommended by the IAEA and for
which no use is foreseen; and
(g) "dumping" means
• (i) any deliberate disposal at sea, including
seabed and subsoil insertion, of radioactive
wastes r o other matter from vessels,
aircraft, platforms r o other man-made
structures at sea, and
(ii) y n a deliberate disposal t a sea, including
seabed and subsoil insertion, of vessels,
aircraft, platforms r o other man-made
structures at sea, containing radioactive
material,
but does not include the disposal of wastes or
other matter incidental to, or derived from the
normal operations of vessels, aircraft, platforms
or other man-made structures at sea and their
equipment, other than wastes or other matter
transported by or to vessels, aircraft, platforms
or other man-made structures t a sea, operating
for the purpose of disposal of such matter or
derived from e h t treatment f o such wastes r o
other matter on such vessels, aircraft, platforms
or structures.

- 4 -
Article 2
APPLICATION OF THE TREATY
1. This Treaty s t i d n a Protocol shall apply e h t o t
territories, continental shelves, and EEZ of the States
Parties within the Zone in which this Treaty in is
force.
2. Nothing n i this Treaty shall prejudice e h t rights
e h t
r o
exercise f o these rights y n a y b State under e h t
provisions e h t f o United Nations Convention w a L e h t n o
of the Sea of 1982, in particular with regard to
freedom of the high seas, rights of innocent passage,
archipelagic a e s lanes passage r o transit passage f o
ships d n a aircraft, d n a consistent with e h t Charter
the
f o
United Nations.
Article 3
BASIC UNDERTAKINGS
1. Each State Party undertakes not to, anywhere inside or
outside the Zone:
(a) develop, manufacture or otherwise acquire,
possess or have control over nuclear weapons;
(b) station r o transport nuclear weapons y n a y b
means; or
(c) test or use nuclear weapons.
2. Each State Party also undertakes o t t o n allow, s t i n i
territory, any other State to:
(a) develop, manufacture or otherwise acquire,
possess or have control over nuclear weapons;
(fa) station nuclear weapons;
) c (
r o
test e s u r o nuclear weapons

- 5 -
Each State Party "also undertakes not to:
(a) dump at sea or discharge into the atmosphere
anywhere within the Zone any radioactive material
or wastes;
) b ( dispose radioactive material r o wastes n o land
e h t
n i
territory r o f o under e h t jurisdiction f o
other States except as stipulated in Paragraph
2(e) of Article 4; or
(c) allow, within its territory, any other State to
dump at sea or discharge into the atmosphere any
radioactive material or wastes.
Each State Party undertakes : o t t o n
(a) seek or receive any assistance in the commission
of in act any violation of the provisions of
Paragraphs 1, 2 and 3 of this Article; or
(b) take any action to assist or encourage the
commission of any act in violation of the
provisions f o Paragraphs 1, f o 3 d n a 2 this
Article. ,
Article 4
USE OF NUCLEAR ENERGY FOR PEACEFUL PURPOSES
Nothing in this Treaty shall prejudice the right of the
States Parties e s u o t nuclear energy, n i particular r o f
their economic development and social progress.
Each State Party therefore undertakes:
e s u o t ) a ( exclusively r o f peaceful purposes nuclear
material and facilities which are within its
territory d n a areas under s t i jurisdiction d n a
control;

- 6 -
(b) prior-to embarking on its peaceful nuclear energy
programme, to subject its programme to rigorous
nuclear safety assessment conforming to
guidelines and standards recommended by the IAEA
for the protection of health and minimisation of
danger o t life d n a property n i accordance with
Paragraph f o 6 Article III e h t f o Statute e h t f o
IAEA;
) c ( upon request, o t make available o t another State
Party the assessment except information relating
to personal data, information protected by
intellectual property rights y b r o industrial r o
commercial confidentiality, and information
relating o t national security;
(d) to support the continued effectiveness of the
international non-proliferation system based
e h t
n o
Treaty e h t n o Non-Proliferation - f o Nuclear
Weapons (NPT) and the IAEA .safeguards system;
o t ) e (
and
dispose radioactive wastes d n a other
radioactive material n i accordance with IAEA
standards and procedures on land within its
territory , or on land within the territory of
another State which has consented to such
disposal.
Each State Party further undertakes o t t o n provide
source or special fissionable material, or equipment or
material especially designed or prepared for the
processing, use or production of special fissionable
material to:
(a) y n a non-nuclear-weapon state except under
conditions subject e h t o t safeguards required y b
Paragraph 1 of Article III of the NPT; ,or
y n a ) b ( nuclear-weapon State except n i conformity
, with applicable safeguards agreements with the
IAEA.

Article 5 '.
IAEA SAFEGUARDS
Each State Party which has not done so shall conclude
an agreement with e h t IAEA e h t r o f application f o full scope
safeguards s t i o t peaceful nuclear activities t o n later than
eighteen months after e h t entry into force r o f that State
Party f o this Treaty.
Article 6
EARLY NOTIFICATION OF A NUCLEAR ACCIDENT
Each State Party which t o n s a h acceded e h t o t
Convention n o Early Notification a f o Nuclear Accident shall
endeavour . o s o d o t
Article 7
FOREIGN SHIPS D N A AIRCRAFT
Each State Party, on being notified, may decide for
itself whether to allow visits by foreign ships and aircraft
to its ports and airfields, transit of its airspace by
foreign aircraft, d n a navigation y b foreign ships through
its territorial sea or archipelagic waters and overflight of
foreign aircraft above those waters a n i manner t o n governed
by e h t rights f o innocent passage, archipelagic a e s lanes
passage or transit passage.
Article 8
ESTABLISHMENT OF THE COMMISSION FOR
THE SOUTHEAST ASIA NUCLEAR WEAPON-FREE 2ONE
There is hereby established a Commission for the
Southeast Asia Nuclear Weapon-Free Zone, hereinafter
referred to as the "Commission".

- 8 -
All States Parties are ipso facto" members of the
Commission. Each State Party shall e b represented y b
its Foreign Minister or his representative accompanied
by alternates and advisers.
e h T function e h t f o Commission shall o t e b oversee e h t
implementation of this Treaty and ensure compliance
with s t i provisions.
The Commission shall meet as and when necessary in
accordance with e h t provisions f o this Treaty including
upon e h t request y n a f o State Party. s a r a f s A
possible, the Commission shall meet in conjunction with
the ASEAN Ministerial Meeting.
At the beginning of each meeting, the Commission shall
elect its Chairman and such other officers as may be
required. They shall hold office until a new Chairman
and other officers are elected at the next meeting.
Unless otherwise provided for in this Treaty, two-
thirds e h t f o members e h t f o Commission shall e b
present o t constitute a quorum.
Each member of the Commission shall have one vote.
Except as provided for in this Treaty, decisions of the
Commission shall be taken by consensus or, failing
consensus, by a two-thirds majority of the members
present and voting.
e h T Commission shall, y b consensus, agree upon d n a
adopt rules of procedure for itself as well as
financial rules governing s t i funding d n a that s t i f o
subsidiary organs.
Article 9
THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE
There s i hereby established, a s a subsidiary organ f o
the Commission, the Executive Committee.

- 9 -
The Executive Committee shall be composed of all States
Parties to this Treaty. Each State Party shall be
represented e n o y b senior official s t i s a
representative, e b y a m o h w accompanied y b alternates
and advisers.
The functions of the Executive Committee shall be to:
) a ( ensure e h t proper operation f o verification
measures in accordance with the provisions on the
Control System as stipulated in Article 10;
(b) consider and decide on requests for clarification
and for a fact-finding mission;
a p u t e s ) c ( fact-finding mission n i accordance with
the Annex of this Treaty;
(d) consider and decide on the findings of a fact-
finding mission and report to the Commission;
(e) request e h t Commission o t convene a meeting when
appropriate and necessary;
(f) conclude such agreements with the IAEA or other
international organizations s a referred n i o t
Article 18 on behalf of the Commission after
being duly authorized to do so by the Commission;
and
(g) carry out such other tasks as may, from time to
time, be assigned by the Commission,
The Executive Committee shall meet as and when
necessary e h t r o f efficient exercise s t i f o functions.
As far as possible, the Executive Committee shall meet
in conjunction with the ASEAN Senior Officials Meeting.
The Chairman of the Executive Committee shall be the
representative e h t f o Chairman e h t f o Commission. y n A
submission or communication made by a State Party to
the Chairman of the Executive Committee shall be
disseminated to the other members of the Executive
Committee.

- 10.-
Two-thirds- of the1-members of the Executive Committee
shall be present to constitute a quorum.
. 7 Each member e h t f o Executive Committee shall have e n o
vote.
8. Decisions of the Executive Committee shall be taken by
consensus or, failing consensus, a y b two-thirds
majority of the members present and voting.
Article 10
CONTROL SYSTEM
;
1. There is hereby established a control system for the
purpose of verifying compliance with the obligations of
the States Parties under this Treaty.
2. The Control System shall comprise:
(a) the IAEA safeguards system as provided for in
Article 5;
(b) report and exchange of information as provided
n i r o f Article 11;
(c) request r o f clarification s a provided n i r o f
Article 12; d n a
(d) request and procedures for a fact-finding mission
as provided for in Article 13.
Article 11
REPORT D N A EXCHANGE F O INFORMATION
Each State Party shall submit reports to the Executive
Committee y n a n o significant event within s t i territory
d n a areas under s t i jurisdiction arid control affecting
the implementation of this Treaty.
The States Parties may exchange information on matters
arising under n i r o relation o t .this Treaty.

Article 12
REQUEST FOR CLARIFICATION
Each State Party shall have the right to request
another State Party for clarification concerning any
situation which may be considered ambiguous or which
may give rise o t doubts about e h t compliance f o that
State Party with this Treaty. t I shall inform e h t
Executive Committee f o such a request. e h T requested
State Party shall duly respond y b providing without
delay the necessary information and inform the
Executive Committee s t i f o reply e h t o t requesting
State Party.
Each State Party shall have the right to request the
Executive Committee to seek clarification from another
State Party concerning y n a situation which
t
e b y a m
considered ambiguous or which may give rise to doubts
about compliance of that State Party with this Treaty.
Upon receipt of such a request/ the Executive Committee
shall consult the State Party from which clarification
is sought for the purpose of obtaining the
clarification requested.
Article 13
REQUEST A R O F FACT-FINDING MISSION
A State Party shal1 have e h t right o t request e h t
Executive Committee to send a fact-finding mission to
another State Party in order to clarify and resolve a
situation which may be considered ambiguous or which may
give rise o t doubts about compliance with e h t provisions f o
this Treaty, in accordance with the procedure contained in
the Annex to this Treaty.

- 12 -
Article 14
REMEDIAL MEASURES
In case the Executive Committee decides in accordance
with the Annex that there is a breach of this Treaty by
a State party, that State Party shall, within a
reasonable time, take all steps necessary to bring
itself in full compliance with this Treaty and shall
promptly inform the Executive Committee of the action
taken r o proposed e b o t taken . t i y b
Where a State Party fails or refuses to comply with the
provisions f o Paragraph f o 1 this Article, e h t
Executive Committee shall request the Commission to
convene a meeting in accordance with the provisions
of Paragraph 3(e) of Article 9.
At e h t meeting convened pursuant o t Paragraph f o 2 this
Article, e h t Commission shall consider e h t emergent
situation and shall decide on any measure it deems
appropriate o t cope with e h t situation, including e h t
submission e h t f o matter e h t o t IAEA and, where e h t
situation might endanger international peace and
security, e h t Security Council e h t d n a General Assembly
of the United Nations.
In the event of breach of the Protocol attached to this
Treaty a y b State Party e h t o t Protocol, e h t Executive
Committee shall convene a special meeting of the
Commission to decide on appropriate measures be to
taken.
Article 5 1
SIGNATURE, RATIFICATION, ACCESSION,
DEPOSIT D N A REGISTRATION
This Treaty shall be open for signature by all States
in Southeast Asia, namely, Brunei Darussaiam, Cambodia,
Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, Philippines,
Singapore, Thailand and Vietnam.

- 13 -.
This . Treaty shall be subject to 'ratification in
accordance with the constitutional procedure of the
signatory States. e h T instruments f o ratification
shall e b deposited with e h t Government e h t f o Kingdom
of Thailand which s i hereby designated e h t s a
Depositary State.
This Treaty shall e b open r o f accession. e h T
instruments of accession shall be deposited with the
Depositary State,
e h T Depositary State shall inform e h t other States
Parties o t this Treaty e h t n o deposit f o instruments f o
ratification or accession.
The Depositary State shall register this Treaty and its
Protocol pursuant to Article 102 of the Charter of the
United Nations.
Article 16
ENTRY INTO FORCE
This Treaty shall enter into force on the date of the
deposit of the seventh instrument of ratification
and/or accession.
For States which ratify or accede to this Treaty after
e h t date f o this seventh instrument f o ratification r o
accession, this Treaty shall enter into force on the
date of deposit its of instrument of ratification or
accession.
Article 17
RESERVATIONS
This Treaty shall not be subject to reservations
•Vf

- 14 -
Article 18
RELATIONS WITH OTHER INTERNATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS
The Commission may conclude such agreements with the
IAEA r o other international organizations t i s a considers
likely to facilitate the efficient operation of the Control
System established by this Treaty.
Article 9 1
AMENDMENTS
1. Any State Party may propose amendments to this Treaty
and its Protocol and shall submit its proposals to the
Executive Committee, which shall transmit them to all
the other States Parties. The Executive Committee shall
immediately request the Commission to convene a meeting
to examine the proposed amendments. The quorum .required
for such a meeting shall be all the members of the
Commission. Any amendment shall be adopted by a
consensus decision of the Commission.
2. Amendments adopted shall enter into force 0 3 days after
the receipt by the Depositary State of the seventh
instrument f o acceptance from e h t states Parties„
Article 0 2
REVIEW
Ten years after this Treaty enters into force, a
meeting e h t f o Commission shall e b convened e h t r o f purpose
of reviewing the operation of this Treaty. A meeting of the
Commission e h t r o f same purpose y a m also e b convened t a
anytime thereafter if there is consensus among all its
members.

- 15 -
- *,'
Article 21
SETTLEMENT F O DISPUTES
Any dispute arising from the interpretation of the
provisions of this Treaty shall be settled by peaceful means
as may be agreed upon by the States Parties to the dispute.
If within one month, the parties to the dispute are unable
to achieve a peaceful settlement of the dispute by
negotiation, mediation, enquiry or conciliation, any of the
parties concerned shall, with e h t prior consent e h t f o
other parties concerned, refer e h t dispute o t arbitration r o
to e h t International Court f o Justice.
Article 22
DURATION AND WITHDRAWAL
This Treaty shall remain in force indefinitely.
In the event of a breach by any State Party of this
Treaty essential e h t o t achievement e h t f o objectives
of this Treaty, every other State Party shall have the
right o t withdraw from this Treaty.
Withdrawal under Paragraph 2 of Article 22, shall be
effected y b giving notice twelve months n i advance o t
the members of the Commission.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, e h t undersigned have signed this Treaty.
DONE at Bangkok, this fifteenth day of December, one
thousand nine hundred and ninety-five, in one original in
e h t English language.

A N N EX
PROCEDURE FOR A F&CT-FXttDING MISSION
The State Party requesting a fact-finding mission as
provided n i Article 13, hereinafter referred e h t s a o t
"requesting State", shall submit the request to the
Executive Committee specifying the following:
(a) the doubts or concerns and the reasons for such
doubts or concerns;
(b) the location in which the situation which gives
rise o t doubts s a h allegedly occurred;
(cj e h t relevant provisions f o this Treaty about
which doubts of compliance have arisen; and
(d) any other relevant information.
Upon receipt of a request for a fact-finding mission,
the Executive Committee shall:
(a) immediately inform the State Party to which the
fact-finding mission is requested to be sent,
hereinafter referred to as the "receiving State",
about the receipt of the request; and
(b) not later than 3 weeks after receiving the
request, decide if the request complies with the
provisions f o Paragraph d n a 1 whether t i t o n r o
is frivolous, abusive or clearly beyond the scope
of this Treaty. Neither the requesting nor
receiving State Party shall participate in such
decisions.
In case e h t Executive Committee decides that e h t
request does not comply with the provisions of
Paragraph 1, or that it is frivolous, abusive or
clearly beyond e h t scope f o this Treaty, t i shall take
no further action e h t n o request d n a inform e h t
requesting State e h t d n a receiving State accordingly.

2 -•
In the event that the Executive Committee decides that
the request complies with the provisions of Paragraph
1, d n a that t o n s i t i frivolous, abusive r o clearly
beyond e h t scope f o this Treaty, t i shall immediately
forward the request for a fact-finding mission to the
receiving State, indicating, inter alia, the proposed
date r o f sending e h t mission. e h T proposed date shall
not be later than 3 weeks from the time the receiving
State receives the request for a fact-finding mission.
The Executive Committee shall also immediately set up
a fact-finding mission consisting 3 f o inspectors from
e h t IAEA e r a o h w neither nationals e h t f o requesting
nor receiving State.
e h T receiving State shall comply with e h t reguest
a
r o f
fact-finding mission referred n i o t Paragraph t I , 4
shall cooperate with the Executive Committee in order
o t facilitate e h t effective functioning e h t f o
fact-finding mission, inter alia, by promptly providing
unimpeded access e h t f o fact-finding mission e h t o t
location in question. The receiving State shall accord
to e h t members e h t f o fact-finding mission such
privileges and immunities as are necessary for them to
exercise their functions effectively, including
inviolability of all papers and documents and immunity
from arrest, detention and.legal process r o f acts done
and words spoken for the purpose of the mission.
The receiving State shall have the right to take
measures o t protect sensitive installations o t d n a
prevent disclosures f o confidential information d n a
data not related to this Treaty.
The fact-finding mission, in the discharge of its
functions, shall:
(a) respect the laws and regulations of the receiving
State;
) b ( refrain from activities inconsistent with e h t
objectives d n a purposes f o this Treaty;
) c ( submit preliminary r o interim reports e h t o t
Executive Committee; and

(d) complete its task without undue delay and shall
submit its final report to the Executive
Committee within a reasonable time upon
completion of its work.
8. The Executive Committee shall:
(a) consider the reports submitted by the fact-
finding mission and reach a decision on whether
or not there is a breach of this Treaty;
(b) immediately communicate s t i decision e h t o t
requesting State and the receiving State; and
) c ( present a full report s t i n o decision e h t o t
Commission.
9. e h t n I event that e h t receiving State refuses o t comply
with ,the request for a fact-finding mission in
accordance with Paragraph 4, the requesting State
through e h t Executive Committee shall have e h t right o t
request for a meeting of the Commission. The Executive
Committee shall immediately request the Commission to
convene a meeting in accordance with Paragraph 3(e) of
Article 9.

PROTOCOL O T
THE TREATY ON SOUTHEAST ASIA
NUCLEAR WEAPON-FREE ZONE
The States Parties to this Protocol,
DESIRING to contribute to efforts towards achieving general
and complete disarmament of nuclear weapons, ,and thereby
ensuring international peace and security, including in
Southeast Asia;
NOTING e h t Treaty e h t n o Southeast Asia Nuclear Weapon-Free
Zone, signed at Bangkok on the fifteenth day of December,
one thousand nine hundred and ninety-five;
HAVE AGREED as follows:
Article 1
Each State Party undertakes o t respect e h t Treaty
e h t
n o
Southeast Asia Nuclear Weapon-Free Zone, hereinafter
referred e th s a o t "Treaty", o t t no d an contribute
t c a
y an o t
which constitutes a violation e h t f o Treaty s t i r o
Protocol by States Parties to them.
Article 2
Each State Party undertakes not to use or threaten to
e s u nuclear weapons against y n a State Party e h t o t Treaty.
It further "undertakes not to use or threaten to use nuclear
weapons within the Southeast Asia Nuclear Weapon-Free Zone.
Article 3
This Protocol shall be open for signature the by
People's'Republic f o China, e h t French Republic, e h t Russian
Federation, e h t United Kingdom f o Great Britain d n a Northern
Ireland e h t d n a United States f o America.

- 2
Article 4
Each State Party undertakes, by written notification to
the Depositary State, o t indicate s t i acceptance r o
otherwise y n a f o alteration s t i o t obligation under this
Protocol that may be brought about by the entry into force
of an amendment to the Treaty pursuant to Article 19
thereof.
Article 5
This Protocol is of a permanent nature and shall remain
in force indefinitely, provided that each State Party shall,
in exercising its national sovereignty, have the right to
withdraw from this Protocol if it decides that extraordinary
events, related e h t o t subject-matter f o this Protocol, have
jeopardized s t i supreme national interests. t I shall give
notice of such withdrawal to the Depositary State twelve
months in advance. Such notice shall include a statement of
the extraordinary events it regards as having jeopardized
its supreme national interests.
Article 6
This Protocol shall e b subject o t ratification.
Article 7
This Protocol shall enter into force r o f each State
Party e h t n o date s t i f o deposit s t i f o instrument f o
ratification with the Depositary State. The Depositary
State shall inform the other States Parties to the Treaty
and o t this Protocol e h t n o deposit f o instruments f o
ratification.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the undersigned, being duly authorized by
their Governments, have signed this Protocol.
DONE at Bangkok this fifteenth day of December, one thousand
nine hundred and ninety-five, in one original in the English
language.
